# shopify_product (Resource)

Manages a Shopify product and its default variant.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "shopify_product" "example" {
  title            = "Example Product"
  handle           = "example-product"
  status           = "DRAFT"
  vendor           = "My Brand"
  product_type     = "Accessories"
  description_html = "<p>An example product managed by Terraform.</p>"
  tags             = ["example", "terraform"]

  price            = "29.99"
  compare_at_price = "39.99"
  sku              = "EXAMPLE-001"
  barcode          = "1234567890123"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `status` (String) Product status: ACTIVE, DRAFT, or ARCHIVED.
- `title` (String) Product title.

### Optional

- `barcode` (String) Default variant barcode (EAN-13).
- `compare_at_price` (String) Default variant compare-at price.
- `description_html` (String) Product description in HTML.
- `handle` (String) URL-friendly product handle. Auto-generated from title if not set.
- `price` (String) Default variant price.
- `product_type` (String) Product type.
- `sku` (String) Default variant SKU.
- `tags` (List of String) Product tags.
- `vendor` (String) Product vendor.

### Read-Only

- `default_variant_id` (String) GID of the default variant created with the product.
- `id` (String) Shopify product GID.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing Shopify product by its GID
terraform import shopify_product.example gid://shopify/Product/123456789
```
